rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Mtn For Mandatory Relief From Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2013, line 4 - DEFENDANT PAUL KRAAIJVANGER and REAL EQUITY GROUP ONE, LLC's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion For Mandatory Relief From Entry Of Default. Defendant's Motion for Reconsideration of Order Denying Motion for Mandatory Relief from Entry of Default is GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part, as follows: (1) The Court will reconsider its prior order based on new fact, specifically, even though courtesy copy provided to the Court did not contain proposed demurrer referenced in paragraph 47 of Karen Uchiyama Declaration, such pleading was attached to the original filed with the Court. (2) The Court DENIES Paul Kraaijvanger's Motion to Set Aside Default. Code of Civil Procedure 473(b) provides that "The court shall, whenever an application for relief is made no more than six months after entry of judgment, is in proper form, and is accompanied by an attorney's sworn affidavit attesting to his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect, vacate any ¿resulting default, ¿unless the court finds that the default or dismissal was not in fact caused by the attorney's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect." The Court does not find that default was caused by attorney mistake, inadvertence, surprise or neglect. If an "'intentional strategic decision'caused the default section 473 relief is not available." Jerry's Shell v. Equilon Enterprises, LLC, (2005)134 Cal. App. 4th 1058, 1073. Newly provided explanation for delay in seeking relief supports Court's finding. Holding that counsel's actions (See Uchiyama Declaration in Support of Motion to Set Side Default paragraph 9-10 and Reply in support of Motion for Reconsideration 2:10-15) were subject to automatic, mandatory relief, would reward and encourage this conduct and render the latter part of the statute quoted above superfluous/meaningless, i.e. "unless the court finds that the default or dismissal was not in fact caused by the attorney's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect." (3) Hearing required re: Defendant's non-compliance with CCP 1005 re: reply. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al

rulings.Case Title: SFDD INVESTMENTS, LLC VS. EDWARD FOY
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2013, line 7 - DEFENDANT EDWARD FOY's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside/Vacate Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain burden under CCP 473(b). Also, Defendant does not deny that on the date judgment was entered he was in breach under the Stipulation. =(501/REQ)
